title: Computer Networks Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of computer networking concepts

questions.questionText: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for routing?
questions.explanation: The Network Layer (Layer 3) is responsible for routing packets between different networks.

questions.questionText: What is the maximum transmission unit (MTU) for Ethernet?
questions.explanation: The standard MTU for Ethernet is 1500 bytes, which is the maximum size of the payload in an Ethernet frame.

questions.questionText: Which protocol is used for reliable data transmission in TCP/IP?
questions.explanation: TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) provides reliable, ordered delivery of data with error checking and flow control.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of ARP (Address Resolution Protocol)?
options: To resolve domain names to IP addresses
To resolve IP addresses to MAC addresses
questions.explanation: ARP resolves IP addresses to MAC (hardware) addresses for communication within the same network segment.

questions.questionText: Which congestion control algorithm is used by TCP?
questions.explanation: TCP uses Slow Start as one of its congestion control algorithms to gradually increase the sending rate.

questions.questionText: What is the default subnet mask for a Class B network?
questions.explanation: Class B networks use a default subnet mask of 255.255.0.0 (/16), providing 16 bits for the network portion.

questions.questionText: Which protocol operates at the application layer and is used for web browsing?
questions.explanation: HTTP (HyperText Transfer Protocol) operates at the application layer and is the foundation of web browsing.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of DHCP?
questions.explanation: DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol) automatically assigns IP addresses and other network configuration parameters to devices.

questions.questionText: Which switching technique has the lowest latency?
questions.explanation: Circuit switching has the lowest latency because a dedicated path is established before data transmission begins.

questions.questionText: What is the maximum number of hosts in a /24 network?
questions.explanation: A /24 network has 8 host bits, allowing 2^8 - 2 = 254 host addresses (excluding network and broadcast addresses).
